name: Kinetic misalignment (axion DM)
summary: Variant of the standard misalignment mechanism for axion DM in which the axion field has nonzero initial velocity (rather than just nonzero initial value). The velocity arises from explicit PQ-breaking in the early universe (typically a higher-dimensional operator active at high T). Allows axion DM with much smaller decay constants f_a ~ 10^7 - 10^9 GeV than the standard window, opening new haloscope-search territory and connecting to axiogenesis.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces Omega_DM h^2 ~ 0.12 for QCD-axion masses up to ~ meV (much higher than the canonical 10 mu eV - 1 meV window of standard misalignment). Predicts axion DM in haloscope-accessible mass ranges.

touches_benchmarks.note: Higher allowed axion mass range opens up coverage by HAYSTAC, ADMX-G2, and proposed CASPEr-type searches.

introduces.note: Builds on PQ axion. New parameters: explicit-breaking operator coefficient and initial radial-mode VEV.

history: Co, Hall, Harigaya (2019), 'Axion kinetic misalignment mechanism', Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 251802, arXiv:1910.14152. Connected to axiogenesis (Co-Harigaya 2020) and to axion-driven inflation. Active 2020-present; complementary haloscope-search programs target the predicted f_a range.

references.citation: Co, Hall, Harigaya, 'Axion kinetic misalignment mechanism', Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 251802 (2020)
